title: Today's AI Brief: Let us filter AI slop, you cowards
summary: A look at the latest AI news, including calls for AI slop filtering and tougher protections against AI-aided bioweapons.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Let us filter AI slop, you cowards
imageCaption: Let us filter AI slop, you cowards (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I've got my coffee in hand, and I'm ready to dive into the latest news from the world of artificial intelligence. Let's get started.

First up, there's a call from some in the tech industry to filter out what's being called 'AI slop.' This refers to the flood of low-quality content being generated by AI tools and posted online. The idea is that platforms like Google, YouTube, Meta, Instagram, and TikTok could give users a filter option to avoid this content, but that would require them to acknowledge the reality of the situation.

Next, AI leaders are sounding the alarm about the potential for AI to be used in developing biological weapons. It's not often that you see rivals in the AI space agreeing on much of anything, but this is one area where there's a unified concern. An open letter to Congress is calling for tougher protections against AI-aided bioweapons.

I'm looking at these developments and wondering what they say about the current state of AI. On one hand, you've got the issue of AI-generated content flooding online platforms. On the other, you've got serious concerns about AI being used for harm. It's clear that AI is a complex and multifaceted technology that's still in the process of being understood.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
